request.description: Authenticate with valid credentials and store accessToken/refreshToken as collection variables for use by protected requests.
Negative test: invalid credentials should be rejected.
Fetch the profile of the currently authenticated user using the {{bearer_token_0137}}
Negative test: request without a valid token should be unauthorized.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair.

Every resource is also reachable behind the /auth/{resource} prefix. Functionally identical to /products, but requires a valid Bearer token; useful for verifying that authenticated and public payloads stay in sync.

item.name: Get Todos As Authenticated User - Negative (garbage token)

request.description: Negative test: the /auth/{resource} prefix must enforce auth the same as /auth/me.

item.description: Login, session, and token-refresh flows. Run first to populate {{accessToken}} for all protected requests.

item.name: Unsupported Verb On Read-Only Nested Route - Negative

request.description: Edge case: DummyJSON's read-only/filter routes are not registered for write verbs. Confirmed live behavior returns 404 (route not found) rather than 405 (method not allowed) - assert the actual contract rather than the 'textbook' expectation.
Simulate deletion of a recipe.

item.description: Recipe CRUD, search, tag and meal-type filtering.

item.name: [Security] XSS payload in new post body

exec: pm.test('Payload echoed back as plain text (not stripped silently, not executed)', function () {

request.description: Security: submits a script-injection payload in a text field; the API should store/echo it as inert text, never execute it, and consuming frontends must escape it on render.
Security: submits an event-handler-based XSS payload; verifies the API does not sanitize/break, so the responsibility for output-encoding is clearly on the frontend.

item.name: [Security][BUG-002] Unauthorized access to /auth/{resource} with tampered token

exec: pm.test('[BUG-002 KNOWN ISSUE] Tampered token handling (ideally 401/403)', function () {
console.warn('KNOWN ISSUE BUG-002: API returns 500 instead of 401/403.');

request.description: Security: verifies a structurally-plausible but invalid JWT is still rejected (not just an empty/missing header).

item.name: [Security] Sensitive fields not exposed in list responses

exec: pm.test('Password field presence is a known/expected part of this demo API only', function () {
// NOTE: DummyJSON intentionally exposes a demo password field for learning purposes;
// in a real production API this assertion should instead be to.not.have.property('password');

request.description: Security: spot-checks that highly sensitive fields (plaintext password) are not casually exposed in bulk list responses beyond what's documented.
